rationale: The cited paper is used in the core theory section to support a key mechanism: effort can reduce guilt and help consumers feel they have earned indulgence. That mechanism directly informs the citing paper's justification-based framing for why hedonic choices vary by decision context. The citation also appears in positioning of prior evidence on hedonic-utilitarian preference reversals, indicating more than a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Kivetz and Simonson (2002a) as conceptual and empirical support for the earned-indulgence/justification mechanism, then develops broader predictions about separate vs joint evaluation and time-vs-money payment preferences. It uses the cited work as a basis rather than replicating the frequency-program setting.
